skillName: Angles in a triangle sum to 180°
misconceptionSummary: Students forget that the three interior angles of any triangle must add up to 180° and guess the missing angle rather than calculating it.

visuals.ruleText: The three interior angles of any triangle add up to 180°
visuals.subText: Works for all triangles — scalene, isosceles, equilateral, right-angled

steps.narration: Every triangle, regardless of shape, has interior angles summing to one hundred and eighty degrees. This holds for all types: right-angled, equilateral, isosceles, scalene. Write the equation: all three angles equal one hundred and eighty.
Triangle with angles fifty, seventy, and x. Equation: fifty plus seventy plus x equals one hundred and eighty. Fifty plus seventy is one hundred and twenty, so x equals sixty degrees.

Right-angled triangle: one angle is ninety, one is thirty-five, find the third. Ninety plus thirty-five plus y equals one hundred and eighty. Y equals fifty-five degrees. The two acute angles in a right-angled triangle always add to ninety.

visuals.label: All triangle angles sum to 180° — subtract known angles from 180° to find missing one

steps.narration: Sixty and fifty-five degrees. Both triangles confirm the rule. Subtract the known angles from one hundred and eighty — do not guess.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: guessing the missing angle or using 360°. Any triangle's angles sum to 180°. Write the equation: known angles + unknown = 180°, then subtract.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: All triangles — every shape and size — have angles summing to 180°. Write the equation, add up the known angles, and subtract from 180°. Never guess when the rule gives an exact answer.
